step_by_step_thinking: I concur with the selection of Option D - Larva Migrans. My agreement hinges on additional insight into its wide-ranging impact beyond local skin manifestations; indeed, systemic symptoms associated with larva migrans include constitutional features such as fever, malaise, and myalgia which are commonly reported alongside cutaneous complaints. Thus, while asymptomatic cases exist, and other less frequent symptoms might occur, the broad spectrum of general symptoms makes Larva Migrans the most frequently encountered systemic issue during larval migration for these parasites. Hence, my final stance aligns perfectly with the group's consensus.
raw_response:
I concur with the selection of Option D - Larva Migrans. My agreement hinges on additional insight into its wide-ranging impact beyond local skin manifestations; indeed, systemic symptoms associated with larva migrans include constitutional features such as fever, malaise, and myalgia which are commonly reported alongside cutaneous complaints. Thus, while asymptomatic cases exist, and other less frequent symptoms might occur, the broad spectrum of general symptoms makes Larva Migrans the most frequently encountered systemic issue during larval migration for these parasites. Hence, my final stance aligns perfectly with the group's consensus.

Answer Choice: **D**.
